feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /b/ Random: if i was technically brain-dead but something else outside of my body was keepin…
entry.content: if i was technically brain-dead but something else outside of my body was keeping me alive, how could i tell? i suspect i may already be dead and might actually be experiencing some form of "psychosomatic reality", as i would call it...a "reality" that technically doesn't exist, but is as good as real to the first-person observer...i also suspect everything i say and do is being recorded somehow

entry.title: /b/ Random: is it possible for someone to do hallucinogens, go on a trip, and become so imme…
entry.content: is it possible for someone to do hallucinogens, go on a trip, and become so immersed in the trip that they actually just become permanently stuck in that new world? how likely is this, and is it something that would even be possible? because i feel as if my whole life up to this point has been some kind of drug trip and i've just been hallucinating this whole time, although i can't prove that.

entry.title: /b/ Random: >got in a fight with the Indian dev on my team >called him a benchod >thought it…

entry.title: /b/ Random: Anti-Communism is making a comeback

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: The government says your wages have never been higher. But I have two Big Macs h…
entry.content: The government says your wages have never been higher. But I have two Big Macs here that disagree. In 2005, the minimum wage was $5.15, and a Big Mac was $2.50, which means an hour of work could buy a Big Mac for you and one for your friend. Fast forward to today. The minimum wage is $7.25, which sounds like an improvement. However, the same burger is now almost $6. So one hour of work today buys you just one Big Mac, so your friend has to go. So the next time you hear the government say wages a

entry.title: /g/ Technology: How to Track Random Lights in the Sky
entry.content: I have seen these flying lights in the sky for almost a decade now. They tend to fly in random directions, so they're definitely not stars or airplanes. >maybe they're helicopters I live in an area with no airports. Also, I check flight radar and there were no helicopters in the area. Are these some type of drone? But my question moreso is how can I get a look at these things? I want to buy a long exposure camera so I can record this. What the fuck are these flying lights that move at weird

entry.title: /int/ International: Russian socialism was incredibly conservative after Stalin came to power. Wester…
entry.content: Russian socialism was incredibly conservative after Stalin came to power. Western society was much, much, much, much more liberal in its customs than the Soviet Union. Abortion was prohibited in the USSR, gays were persecuted, and men could beat their wives and the Soviet state would do nothing.

entry.title: /b/ Random: Now that I’ve been off /b/ for a while, I thought I could walk anonymously throu…
entry.content: Now that I’ve been off /b/ for a while, I thought I could walk anonymously through a store. It worked at first, but then someone yelled, “Hey, it’s Andy Sixx!” Then I was surrounded by people. “How are you?” “We miss you!” It was a bit of a circus. I’m almost sorry I yelled.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Jazz is Shit
entry.content: I was listening to Bitches Brew by Miles Davis. More like Shit's Poo. Just endless solos like in prog rock. Electric guitars playing randomly instead of epic riffs. And this is considered one of the best. Utterly insane. John McLaughlin makes me LAUGH with how shit he is. I also listened to My Favorite Things by John Coltrane, just the same 2 minutes over and over. And this was acclaimed? I thought maybe these are just two bad selections. I listened to Kind of Blue and it was just nonsense, no r

entry.title: /int/ International: >no tenses >no gendered noun >no plural forms >no definite/indefinite articles >…

entry.title: /int/ International: Is Brazil the most pathetic cuck soccer playing nation in the world? When other …
entry.content: Is Brazil the most pathetic cuck soccer playing nation in the world? When other teams lose you’re either not surprised or feel bad for them, but with Brazil you can’t help but laugh. I think it’s about time Brazilians start identifying with the DBZ character who best represents them and hand Goku over to a team that deserve him.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Of all the real, proven threats to humanity, none of them have killed as many pe…
entry.content: Of all the real, proven threats to humanity, none of them have killed as many people as government itself. In academia, it's known as democide. But notice, you've probably never heard that, even if you went to college. And you've certainly never seen it on the news or on the Discovery Channel. "Oh no, they're too busy telling you about sharks. They're going to get you any minute with Shark Week and Bin Laden and... We gotta stick our hands down your pants." No, you never hear about democide, and

entry.content: "Apologies to everyone who came to Roskilde festival hoping to see us play a full set last night. Pop diva Damon Albarn of the novelty cartoon act Gorillaz threw a hissy fit while we played, threatened to storm off stage before finally getting our set shut down because we were "too loud".
